message.content:
Okay! Imagine your favorite toy. If you take really good care of it, it will last a long time and still look nice. But if you keep throwing it around or let it bump into other toys, it might get some scratches or even break.

Now, think of a sharp knife as that favorite toy. When you wash it by hand, you're being careful and gentle, just like when you're playing nicely with your toy. You use a soft sponge, and everything is slow and safe.

But when you put the knife in the dishwasher, it's like putting your toy into a wild ride where it bumps and knocks into other dishes. The dishwasher sprays water really hard, which can make the knife hit other things, and there's strong hot water and soap that can also be rough on your knife's sharp edge.

So, even though it's just water and soap, the action in the dishwasher is much more intense than when you wash by hand. This can make the knife dull or even damage it over time, just like how a rough ride can damage your toy. That's why it's better to wash sharp knives by hand to keep them in good shape!
